visual_features.overall_style: Old‑generation corporate website with a boxed, fixed‑width layout. Visually conservative, skeuomorphic touches (beveled bars, gradients, patterned background). Colorful but not vibrant; primarily blue‑white with red highlights. Feels utilitarian and information-heavy rather than marketing-driven.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven mainly by color, boldness, and horizontal rules. The logo strip and photo banner form the dominant top band. Below, the horizontal navigation bar is the second focal area (blue links, red active item). Inside the content, blue section titles with icons stand out most, followed by bolded red text in the sidebar and inline links. Body text is uniform and dense, so sub-hierarchy inside long paragraphs is weak; thumbnails in rows provide visual anchors further down.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is limited. Content sits in a fixed central column with narrow internal paddings (≈6–10px). Columns are tightly packed, and paragraphs have small line spacing and little vertical separation. Section headers have slightly more margin above/below and are separated by colored rules, but overall the layout is compact and information-dense, prioritizing maximum content per viewport over breathing room.
unique_elements: Patterned blue outer background with diagonal diamond texture framing a centered white content box
Top banner combining logo typography with several industrial facility photos aligned horizontally
Horizontal navigation where the active item is rendered in red while others are blue, under a colored rule
Left sidebar composed of stacked, card-like strips (search, motto, news) with pale blue backgrounds and red emphasized text
Inline breadcrumb and small printer icon in the content area header, hinting at a print-friendly layout

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width grid, approximately 2-column layout within a centered 900–980px container. Not a modern 12-column system, but conceptually a main-content column (~70–75%) and a left sidebar (~25–30%) with occasional full-width header and navigation rows.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic boxed layout: full-width patterned background > centered white page container > stacked horizontal bands (logo/banner, navigation) > two-column content area (left sidebar + main article) > full-width footer (not fully visible but implied).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-to-bottom stacking of major sections: (1) patterned background framing the page, (2) logo and facility images banner, (3) horizontal navigation bar, (4) content region with sidebar and main reference content, (5) repeated article sections with headings, text and image rows. Within the main content, reference entries follow a repeated pattern: heading with icon, body text paragraph block, then a row of thumbnails.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned content. The entire page container is centered horizontally in the viewport. Inside, both sidebar and main content align to a shared vertical grid line. Text is left-aligned; headings sit flush to the left margin inside their columns. Navigation items are inline/inline-block, left-aligned as a row. Most elements respect a consistent internal left padding, creating a visual column edge.
responsive_hints: Design appears purely fixed-width with no visible responsive behavior; widths and columns look rigid.
Side-by-side columns and dense content suggest that, on smaller viewports, horizontal scrolling would occur rather than reflowing.
Navigation is horizontal text links with no sign of mobile menu treatment.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom stacking of major bands, with a persistent two-column interior. Specifically: a full-width logo and imagery strip at the top; directly below, a full-width navigation bar; then a two-column band where the left column is a stacked set of smaller blocks (search field, motto, news items with thumbnails) and the right column contains the main text article. Within the main article column, content flows vertically: each reference entry begins with a blue heading line and icon, followed by one or more dense paragraphs, then a single-row strip of small photos; the pattern repeats for each subsequent reference.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Roughly 15–20% of the visible page height is used by the logo and banner imagery, another 5–8% by the navigation bar. The remaining 70–80% is taken by the two-column content area, of which about three-quarters width is main content and one-quarter is sidebar. Vertical spacing between modules is modest (≈8–16px). Header and navigation occupy full width; sidebar consumes the full height of the content column with stacked boxes; main column is mostly text with occasional image rows occupying the full column width.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Overall, a compact, information-dense design. Paragraphs are long with minimal spacing between them. Sidebar blocks have small paddings and many links packed closely together. Headings and image rows slightly break the density but do not introduce large breathing areas. The result is utilitarian: focused on presenting as much reference text and news as possible in one view.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary flow is a traditional F-pattern: the eye starts at the logo and company name at the top left, scans right across the banner images, then moves down to the horizontal navigation and scans left-to-right again. From there, focus drops diagonally into the main content heading 'Flotation graphite' (bold blue), while peripherally catching the left sidebar items. Reading then proceeds vertically down through the long paragraphs, interrupted sideways by the row of images, then continues down to the next reference heading. The red-colored active navigation item and red-highlighted text in the sidebar serve as secondary attractors along this path.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons (e.g., search) appear as simple rectangular elements with sharp corners (0px radius) and a subtle 1px border in medium gray (#999–#BBB). The search field is paired with a small adjacent rectangular button likely with a light gray fill and a darker border, flat rather than 3D. No visible icons beyond text inside buttons. The style is largely flat with minimal shading, consistent with early 2000s form controls.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely standard HTML <form> with <input type="text"> and <input type="submit"> or <button> for the search control. Other CTA-like interactions are mostly rendered as text links (<a>) styled with color changes rather than custom button elements.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar sections (Motto, News) behave like cards: full-width strips with a pale blue background (#E5ECF5), thin 1px border in a slightly darker gray/blue, and internal padding around 8–10px. Corners are square, no radius. There is no drop shadow; separation is purely via background color and border. Titles inside these blocks may be bold red or blue text at the top with content below. In the main column, each reference section is implicitly card-like, separated by horizontal lines and spacing rather than fully boxed borders.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Search form is minimalistic: a rectangular input with white background, thin 1px medium-gray border, and compact height (≈24–28px). Corners are sharp. Placeholder or prefilled text is small, matching body font. Labels are not visually prominent; the field is self-explanatory (with 'search...' as placeholder text). Form density is high, with minimal padding around fields and no spacious grouping.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Forms are probably simple, single-row or stacked forms with labels inline or above in plain text. Layout is likely achieved via basic HTML forms (
,

ui_patterns.visual_description: Main navigation is a horizontal text menu below the banner. Each item is plain text (e.g., Home, Products, Services for, References, etc.) with a strong blue color (#003399) and bold or semi-bold weight. The active page item ('References') is colored red (#CC0000) and may drop the underline or alter state slightly to stand out. Items are separated by consistent horizontal padding but no visible vertical separators. The nav bar sits atop a colored horizontal rule, giving the impression of a menu strip. Hover states are not visible in the screenshot, but likely involve underlining or color change.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most likely built as an unordered list (<ul><li><a>), styled horizontally using float/inline-block, or as a sequence of <a> elements within a container. Active state is applied via a CSS class on the current page’s <a> or <li>. Breadcrumb navigation appears separately in the main content area as a small 'Home > References' text, implemented as inline <a> elements separated by '>' characters.

ui_patterns.visual_description: News items and reference entries behave like lists. In the sidebar, news links are stacked vertically with line breaks and occasional small thumbnails beneath text. Text is compact, often with the headline in bold or colored link style followed by a short description. No bullet glyphs are visible; separation is through line breaks and slight spacing. In the main content, reference sections follow one after another, separated by heading, paragraphs, and rows of thumbs. There are no strong dividers between list items aside from spacing and occasional thin rules.

    elements forming an implicit list. Image rows are probably implemented as consecutive tags inside a parent container, relying on their natural inline alignment or on simple table/flex-like behavior via CSS or in older code.

heading.visual_style: Sans-serif, likely Arial or Helvetica: neutral, slightly condensed, with simple strokes and moderate x-height. Headings appear in bold weights and are colored blue to stand out.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif, probably default system font (Arial/Helvetica). Reads as a generic corporate text face—no high contrast or humanist characteristics.

font_sizes.h1: 26–30px (banner/logo apart; main content top heading approximately 26px)
font_sizes.body: 12–13px (normal paragraph and link text, fairly small by modern standards)

spacing_system.component_padding: Internal padding for cards/sections is around 8–12px on all sides. Navigation items have horizontal padding of ~8–12px and minimal vertical padding (~4–6px). Input fields have around 4–6px internal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between reference blocks or between sidebar modules) is approximately 12–16px, with some narrower separations (~8px) for tightly related elements such as paragraphs inside the same section.

interaction_hints: Main navigation links likely change text color or underline on hover; active state is emphasized by red coloring as shown.
Body and sidebar links (especially news items) likely use an underline on hover and possibly a visited link color slightly darker or more purple-toned than default blue.
Thumbnail images may act as links to larger images or detail pages; hover effects might be simple cursor change to pointer with no visual transition.

Transitions and animations appear absent in this static design; any micro‑interactions would be instantaneous state changes rather than animated (no visible drop shadows or motion cues). For modernization, subtle fade or color-transition on navigation and image hover (150–200ms) would be consistent but not overwhelming.
